description: Google's Search isn't a search engine anymore. At I/O 2026, the company officially rebuilt its 25-year-old product around Gemini 3.5 Flash—a box that now accepts photos, files, and video, generates custom interactive pages on the fly, and deploys AI agents to monitor the web indefinitely on your behalf. With AI Overviews at 2.5 billion monthly users and AI Mode crossing one billion, Google isn't pitching a future. It's documenting a shift that already happened.

description: Medipally police have filed a case against an Uppal resident and his family for dowry harassment and assault. A London-returned IT professional accused them of demanding additional dowry, threatening her, and assaulting her, leading to her hospitalization. This follows an earlier domestic violence case registered against the accused.

Army makes its artillery arm more lethal by giving its guns automatic aiming capability
description: The Indian Army is enhancing its 105mm field guns with automatic aiming technology, a significant modernization step. This computer-driven system drastically reduces manual effort, improving accuracy, response times, and survivability for artillery crews. This upgrade aligns with the broader Field Artillery Rationalisation Plan, aiming for a more potent and efficient artillery force.

Taylor Swift and Blake Lively feud takes new turn before Kelce wedding
description: Taylor Swift and popular Hollywood actress Blake Lively, have remained distant from one another for more than a year now after the pop star was dragged into the lawsuit with Justin Baldoni. As guests are being invited just weeks ahead of Taylor Swift’s lavish wedding to the Kansas City Chiefs’ star player, Travis Kelce, it seems the billionaire pop star has made her feelings clear about Blake Lively.

title: 'Doubted child’s paternity': Man kills father, wife over suspected affair
description: A shocking incident unfolded in Bulandshahr where a 28-year-old man allegedly murdered his father and wife, driven by suspicions of an illicit affair. The man reportedly used his father's licensed pistol after a heated argument. Police have arrested the accused, who claimed to have found incriminating evidence of the alleged relationship.

title: The heat that hurts, the rain that heals: Inside India’s monsoon machine
description: Delhi's intense summer heat, pushing past 45°C, is not just a discomfort but a vital part of a natural engine. This brutal heat drives the monsoon, aids soil health, ripens fruits, and suppresses pests, playing a crucial role in India's agricultural and ecological cycles.

title: Bengaluru sees 1 economic offence every 2.5 hours
description: Bengaluru faces a surge in economic crimes. Over three years, one offense occurred every 2.5 hours. Forgery, cheating, and fraud dominate these cases. This indicates a shift from physical theft to financial deception. Police face challenges due to cross-state networks and digital methods. The city's crime profile is changing.

title: Jeff Bezos to Amazon users: 'We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system'
description: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
description: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.
